record_identity.title: Client-specific conversation and authentication failure presented through HTTP 403

summary: OpenAI reported a resolved 12 July 2026 incident affecting ChatGPT on iOS and macOS. The incident initially presented as HTTP 403 errors when some iOS users attempted to load or use conversations, then expanded to conversation, sign-up, sign-in, account-creation, and password-update errors across iOS and macOS. OpenAI applied mitigations and reported full recovery. The official incident record does not identify the underlying technical cause.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: HTTP 403 ordinarily communicates an authorization or permission denial. In this incident it was used at the user-facing boundary during a broader client-specific availability and authentication failure. This creates a governance-signalling risk: users may infer account enforcement, policy restriction, entitlement denial, or security action when the operative state is client, session, authentication, or service degradation. The observation therefore supports separation of authorization denial, authentication failure, client-runtime failure, entitlement resolution, and service unavailability within access-state governance.

source_records.source_title: Elevated conversation and login errors on ChatGPT for iOS and macOS
source_records.deployment_context: Consumer ChatGPT client applications on iOS and macOS, including conversation access, sign-up, sign-in, account creation, and password update flows.
source_records.source_context: OpenAI first reported that some iOS users could encounter 403 errors while loading or using ChatGPT conversations. Later updates identified elevated conversation, sign-up, and sign-in errors affecting iOS and macOS, followed by account-creation and password-update errors. OpenAI reported that mitigations restored conversation and sign-in access and marked the incident resolved at 3:28 PM on 12 July 2026.
source_records.relevance_note: Official evidence of a client-specific access incident where an authorization-coded user-facing error appeared during broader conversation and authentication degradation.

system_context.specific_model_or_runtime: ChatGPT iOS and macOS clients; underlying model and backend component not specified
system_context.deployment_context: Consumer AI service accessed through platform-specific client applications.
system_context.affected_population: some ChatGPT users on iOS and macOS

secondary_jurisdictions: paid and free ChatGPT service contexts
jurisdictional_context.sector: frontier AI platforms / consumer AI services / mobile and desktop applications

possible_taxonomy_mapping.candidate_failure_description: A client or platform presents an authorization-coded error during authentication, client-runtime, entitlement-resolution, or service-availability degradation without preserving the operative access-state distinction.
possible_taxonomy_mapping.classification_limit: The official source confirms the affected surfaces and the initial 403 presentation but does not disclose the technical root cause or establish that all affected users saw the same error state.

related_or_similar_instruments: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-04 — Annex B: Arbitration Layer & Resolution Model
CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 — Appendix B: Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy
cam_internal.governance_layer: access-state governance / user-facing error semantics / client and session diagnostics / service-state disclosure
routing_note: Treat this as fresh evidence for FM-0008 rather than as a new confirmed failure mode.
The observation supports a distinction between authorization denial, authentication failure, entitlement resolution failure, client-runtime failure, session failure, and service unavailability.
Do not infer account enforcement, policy restriction, or security action from the HTTP 403 presentation without separate evidence.
cam_internal.validator_or_automation_impact: generated observation and master registry indexes require rebuild

observation_status.recurrence_status: single official incident with multiple affected access and account surfaces
observation_status.resolution_status: platform incident resolved; root cause not publicly disclosed
observation_status.observed_pattern: A client-specific ChatGPT incident initially surfaced through HTTP 403 conversation errors and later encompassed conversation, sign-up, sign-in, account-creation, and password-update failures.
observation_status.repair_linkage: FM-0008 and PATCH-0005 already define and repair the CAM access-state governance gap; this incident supplies post-patch external recurrence evidence.
observation_status.monitoring_note: Monitor for a post-incident explanation, recurring client-specific authorization-coded errors, persistent account effects, conversation loss, or product-surface divergence.

next_action: Add this incident to FM-0008 evidence review, confirm whether any post-incident explanation distinguishes client, session, authentication, authorization, entitlement, and service states, and assess whether VIGIL requires a dedicated diagnostic-error-semantics subtype after further recurrence.
